target.name: D-arabino-phytosphingosine 244
target.motivation: D-ribo-Phytosphingosine exhibits cytotoxic activity against a cell line derived from human leukemia and also serves as an indicator of thermal stress in yeast cells. Several analogues of D-ribo-phytosphingosine have been observed to demonstrate antitumor, antiviral and immunostimulatory activities.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The synthesis of D-arabino-phytosphingosine 244 was achieved through a diastereoselective cross-benzoin reaction followed by reduction and deprotection steps. The key step involved the NHC-catalyzed cross-benzoin reaction between an aliphatic aldehyde and an N-Boc-protected amino aldehyde.

thought.background: D-arabino-phytosphingosine is a biologically active molecule. A diastereoselective synthesis is desired.
thought.gap: A method for the diastereoselective formation of the alpha-hydroxyketone precursor is needed.
thought.decision: To form the alpha-hydroxyketone, a cross-benzoin reaction between aliphatic aldehyde 239 and N-Boc-protected amino aldehyde 240 was chosen, catalyzed by triazolium-based NHC 241, expecting a diastereomeric mixture.

outcome.observation: The cross-benzoin reaction between aliphatic aldehyde 239 and N-Boc-protected amino aldehyde 240 in the presence of triazolium-based NHC 241 furnished a mixture of 242 with 5:1 diastereoselectivity ratio.

evidence.text_span: The total synthesis began with the cross-benzoin reaction between aliphatic aldehyde 239 and N-Boc-protected amino aldehyde 240 in the presence of triazolium-based NHC 241 (catalyst) to furnish a mixture of 242 with 5 : 1 diastereoselectivity ratio.

thought.background: The alpha-hydroxyketone 242 has been synthesized as a diastereomeric mixture.
thought.gap: The ketone needs to be reduced to an alcohol, and the diastereoselectivity needs to be improved.
thought.decision: To reduce the ketone and improve diastereoselectivity, the diastereomeric mixture 242 was reduced with ZnCl2/NaBH4, expecting a higher diastereomeric ratio for the amino diols.

outcome.observation: The diastereomeric mixture 242 was reduced with ZnCl2/NaBH4 to generate the amino diols 243 with >20:1 dr.

evidence.text_span: In the next step, the diastereomeric mixture 242 was reduced with ZnCl2/NaBH4 to generate the amino diols 243 with > 20 : 1 dr.

thought.background: The amino diol 243 has been synthesized with high diastereoselectivity.
thought.gap: The protecting groups (Boc and BOM) need to be removed to obtain the final natural product.
thought.decision: To obtain the final natural product, amino diol 243 was subjected to deprotection using HCl/MeOH.

outcome.observation: Amino diol 243 was subjected to deprotection to furnish the final natural product D-arabino-phytosphingosine 244 in 59% yield.

evidence.text_span: Finally, amino diol 243 was subjected to deprotection to furnish the nal natural product D-arabino-phytosphingosine 244 (Scheme 26).

validation.significance: This synthesis provides a diastereoselective route to D-arabino-phytosphingosine, a derivative of D-ribo-phytosphingosine, which exhibits various biological activities including antitumor, antiviral, and immunostimulatory effects. The use of NHC catalysis in the key cross-benzoin reaction highlights its utility in asymmetric synthesis of complex natural products.

source.title: NHC-catalyzed asymmetric synthesis of natural products and pharmaceutical drugs.
source.authors: Shahid A, Khan SG, Saif MJ, Zahoor AF, Parveen B, Mushtaq A, Mansha A, Mermer A, Chaudhry AR, Irfan A.
